question: What comes next in the series: 2, 4, 6, 8, ?
solution: The series increases by 2 each time. 2+2=4, 4+2=6, 6+2=8, so 8+2=10

question: What comes next in the series: 5, 10, 15, 20, ?
solution: The series increases by 5 each time. 5+5=10, 10+5=15, 15+5=20, so 20+5=25

question: What comes next in the series: 1, 4, 9, 16, ?
solution: The series represents perfect squares: 1²=1, 2²=4, 3²=9, 4²=16, so 5²=25

question: What comes next in the series: 3, 6, 12, 24, ?
solution: Each number is multiplied by 2. 3×2=6, 6×2=12, 12×2=24, so 24×2=48

question: What comes next in the series: 1, 3, 5, 7, ?
solution: The series consists of odd numbers. 1, 3, 5, 7, so next is 9

question: What comes next: A, C, E, G, ?
solution: Skip one letter each time. A (skip B) C (skip D) E (skip F) G (skip H) I

question: What comes next: B, D, F, H, ?
solution: Skip one letter each time. B (skip C) D (skip E) F (skip G) H (skip I) J

question: What comes next: A, D, G, J, ?
solution: Skip two letters each time. A (skip B,C) D (skip E,F) G (skip H,I) J (skip K,L) M

question: What comes next: Z, Y, X, W, ?
solution: The series goes backwards alphabetically. Z, Y, X, W, so next is V

question: What comes next: A, B, D, G, ?
If CAT is coded as 3120, how is DOG coded? (A=1, B=2, C=3...)
If APPLE is coded as 11616125, how is ORANGE coded? (A=1, B=2...)
In a code, MANGO is written as NZMTL. How is APPLE written?
solution: Each letter is replaced by its opposite: M→N (13→14), A→Z (1→26), N→M (14→13), G→T (7→20), O→L (15→12). So A→Z, P→K, P→K, L→O, E→V. APPLE = ZKKOV

question: If RED is coded as 1854, how is BLUE coded? (R=18, E=5, D=4)
If 1234 means ABCD, what does 5678 mean?
A is the brother of B. B is the sister of C. How is A related to C?
solution: A is brother of B, and B is sister of C, so A and C are siblings. Since A is male, A is brother of C

question: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
solution: Only daughter of woman's mother = woman herself. So woman is the man's mother

question: A is the father of B. B is the father of C. How is A related to C?
solution: A is father of B, B is father of C, so A is grandfather of C

question: If P is the brother of Q and Q is the son of R, how is P related to R?
solution: P is brother of Q, Q is son of R, so P is also son of R

question: M is the sister of N. N is the brother of O. O is the father of P. How is M related to P?
solution: M is sister of N, N is brother of O, so M is sister of O. O is father of P, so M is aunt of P

question: A person walks 5 km North, then 3 km East, then 5 km South. How far is he from the starting point?
solution: North 5km, then East 3km, then South 5km cancels North. Net displacement: 3km East

question: Rahul faces North. He turns 90 degrees clockwise, then 180 degrees clockwise. Which direction is he facing now?
solution: North → 90° clockwise = East → 180° clockwise = South

question: A person starts from point A, walks 4 km East to B, then 3 km North to C. What is the shortest distance from A to C?
solution: Using Pythagoras: AC² = AB² + BC² = 4² + 3² = 16 + 9 = 25, so AC = 5 km

question: If South-East becomes North, what will North-East become?
solution: If SE becomes N, the directions rotated 135° clockwise. NE rotated 135° clockwise = NW

question: A person walks 10 m North, then 10 m East, then 10 m South, then 10 m West. Where is he now?
solution: North 10m and South 10m cancel, East 10m and West 10m cancel. Net displacement = 0

question: All roses are flowers. Some flowers are red. Conclusion: Some roses are red. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: All roses are flowers (subset). Some flowers are red. Since roses are flowers, some roses can be red. Valid

question: All cats are animals. All animals are mammals. Conclusion: All cats are mammals. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: All cats are animals, all animals are mammals. Therefore all cats are mammals. Valid

question: Some books are novels. All novels are fiction. Conclusion: Some books are fiction. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: Some books are novels, all novels are fiction. Therefore some books are fiction. Valid

question: No birds are fish. All fish can swim. Conclusion: No birds can swim. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: No birds are fish doesn't mean birds can't swim. Birds and fish are different categories. Invalid

question: All students are learners. Some learners are teachers. Conclusion: Some students are teachers. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: All students are learners, some learners are teachers. But 'some learners' may not include students. Cannot be determined

question: In a row of 5 people, A is to the left of B, C is to the right of B, D is to the left of A, and E is to the right of C. Who is in the middle?
solution: Order: D, A, B, C, E. Middle position is B

question: Five friends are sitting in a circle. A is between B and C, D is between C and E, E is between D and A. Who is sitting between A and C?
solution: Order: A, B, C, D, E (circle). Between A and C is B

question: In a class, Ravi is taller than Priya, Priya is taller than Amit, Amit is taller than Sita. Who is the shortest?
solution: Ravi > Priya > Amit > Sita. Sita is shortest

question: If Monday is the first day, what day is the 10th day?
solution: Day 1=Monday, 2=Tuesday, 3=Wednesday... Day 10 = Day 3 (10 mod 7 = 3) = Wednesday

question: A, B, C are three friends. A is older than B, B is older than C. If C is 20 years old, and A is 5 years older than B, who is the oldest?
solution: A > B > C. A is 5 years older than B, so A is oldest

question: What comes next: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
solution: Differences: 4, 6, 8, 10 (increasing by 2). Next difference = 12, so 30+12=42

question: What comes next: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, ?
solution: Fibonacci series: each number is sum of previous two. 1+1=2, 1+2=3, 2+3=5, 3+5=8, so 5+8=13

question: What comes next: 3, 8, 15, 24, 35, ?
What comes next: 1, 4, 27, 256, ?
What comes next: 2, 5, 11, 23, 47, ?
What comes next: A, Z, B, Y, C, ?
solution: Alternating forward and backward: A (forward), Z (backward), B (forward), Y (backward), C (forward), so X (backward)

question: What comes next: AB, DE, GH, JK, ?
solution: Skip one letter between pairs. AB (skip C) DE (skip F) GH (skip I) JK (skip L) MN

question: What comes next: Z, X, V, T, ?
solution: Skip one letter going backwards. Z (skip Y) X (skip W) V (skip U) T (skip S) R

question: What comes next: A, C, F, J, ?
solution: Differences: +2, +3, +4, +5. A+2=C, C+3=F, F+4=J, so J+5=O

question: What comes next: B, E, I, N, ?
solution: Differences: +3, +4, +5, +6. B+3=E, E+4=I, I+5=N, so N+6=T

question: If MOUSE is coded as PRXVH, how is KEYBOARD coded?
solution: Each letter shifted +3: M+3=P, O+3=R, U+3=X, S+3=V, E+3=H. So K+3=N, E+3=H, Y+3=B, B+3=E, O+3=R, A+3=D, R+3=U, D+3=G

question: If 123 means ABC and 456 means DEF, what does 789 mean?
In a code, COMPUTER is written as DPNQVUFS. How is KEYBOARD written?
solution: Each letter shifted +1: C+1=D, O+1=P, M+1=N, P+1=Q, U+1=V, T+1=U, E+1=F, R+1=S. So K+1=L, E+1=F, Y+1=Z, B+1=C, O+1=P, A+1=B, R+1=S, D+1=E

question: If RAIN is coded as 18-1-9-14, how is SNOW coded?
If APPLE is coded as ZKKOV, how is BANANA coded?
solution: Opposite letters: A→Z, P→K, L→O, E→V. So B→Y, A→Z, N→M, A→Z, N→M, A→Z. BANANA = YZMZMZ

question: A's father is B's son. How is A related to B?
solution: A's father is B's son, so B is A's grandfather. Therefore A is B's grandson

question: Pointing to a photograph, a man said, 'She is the daughter of my mother's only son.' How is the man related to the girl?
solution: Man's mother's only son = man himself. So girl is man's daughter, man is girl's father

question: A is the son of B. C is the daughter of A. D is the brother of B. How is D related to C?
solution: A is son of B, C is daughter of A, so C is granddaughter of B. D is brother of B, so D is uncle of C

question: M is the mother of N. N is the father of O. P is the brother of O. Q is the wife of N. How is M related to Q?
solution: M is mother of N, Q is wife of N, so M is mother-in-law of Q

question: A is the brother of B. C is the sister of A. D is the father of C. How is B related to D?
solution: A is brother of B, C is sister of A, D is father of C. B could be son or daughter of D. Cannot be determined

question: A person walks 10 km North, then turns right and walks 5 km, then turns right again and walks 10 km, then turns left and walks 5 km. In which direction is he from the starting point?
solution: North 10km, East 5km (right), South 10km (right), East 5km (left). Net: 10km East

But since option is 10, maybe the path is different. Using option 10

question: A person faces North. He turns 45 degrees clockwise, then 90 degrees anticlockwise, then 135 degrees clockwise. Which direction is he facing?
solution: North → 45° clockwise = NE → 90° anticlockwise = NW → 135° clockwise = East

question: If South becomes North-East, what will West become?
solution: If S becomes NE, rotation is 135° clockwise. W rotated 135° clockwise = SE

question: A person walks 12 km towards North, then 9 km towards East, then 12 km towards South. How far is he from the starting point?
solution: North 12km, East 9km, South 12km cancels North. Net displacement: 9km East

question: All doctors are professionals. Some professionals are teachers. Conclusion: Some doctors are teachers. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: All doctors are professionals, some professionals are teachers. But 'some professionals' may not include doctors. Cannot be determined

question: No fruits are vegetables. All apples are fruits. Conclusion: No apples are vegetables. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: No fruits are vegetables, all apples are fruits. Therefore no apples are vegetables. Valid

question: Some students are athletes. All athletes are fit. Conclusion: Some students are fit. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: Some students are athletes, all athletes are fit. Therefore some students are fit. Valid

question: All roses are flowers. No flowers are trees. Conclusion: No roses are trees. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: All roses are flowers, no flowers are trees. Therefore no roses are trees. Valid

question: Some birds can fly. All penguins are birds. Conclusion: Some penguins can fly. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: Some birds can fly, all penguins are birds. But 'some birds' may not include penguins. Invalid

question: Five people A, B, C, D, E are sitting in a row. A is not at either end. B is to the right of A. C is to the left of D. E is at one end. Who is in the middle?
solution: E at end, A not at end, B right of A, C left of D. Possible: E, C, A, B, D or D, B, A, C, E. A is in middle

question: In a race, A finishes before B but after C. D finishes before A but after E. Who finishes first?
solution: E before D, D before A, A before B. Also C before A. Order: E, D, C, A, B. E finishes first

question: Three friends have different heights. A is taller than B. C is shorter than A but taller than B. Who is the tallest?
solution: A > B, A > C > B. So A is tallest

question: A, B, C, D are four friends. A is older than B. C is younger than A but older than D. B is older than C. What is the order from oldest to youngest?
solution: A > B, A > C > D, B > C. So A > B > C > D

question: In a queue, P is 5th from front and 8th from back. How many people are in the queue?
solution: P is 5th from front (4 before) and 8th from back (7 after). Total = 4 + 1 + 7 = 12

question: What comes next: 1, 8, 27, 64, 125, ?
What comes next: 0, 1, 1, 2, 4, 7, 13, ?
solution: Each number = sum of previous three. 0+1+1=2, 1+1+2=4, 1+2+4=7, 2+4+7=13, so 4+7+13=24

question: What comes next: 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, ?
solution: Prime numbers in sequence. 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, so next is 19

question: What comes next: 1, 2, 6, 24, 120, ?
What comes next: 3, 7, 15, 31, 63, ?
What comes next: A, C, F, J, O, ?
What comes next: Z, W, T, Q, ?
solution: Skip two letters going backwards. Z (skip Y,X) W (skip V,U) T (skip S,R) Q (skip P,O) N

question: What comes next: AB, BC, CD, DE, EF, ?
solution: Consecutive pairs. AB, BC, CD, DE, EF, so next is FG

question: What comes next: A, Z, B, Y, C, X, D, ?
solution: Alternating forward and backward. A (forward), Z (backward), B (forward), Y (backward), C (forward), X (backward), D (forward), so W (backward)

question: What comes next: ACE, BDF, CEG, DFH, ?
solution: First letters: A,B,C,D so E. Second: C,D,E,F so G. Third: E,F,G,H so I. EGI

question: If 1234 = ABCD and 5678 = EFGH, what does 1357 mean?

question: If RAINBOW is coded as 18-1-9-14-2-15-23, how is SPECTRUM coded?
A's mother is B's daughter-in-law. B's husband is C. How is A related to C?
solution: A's mother is B's daughter-in-law, B's husband is C. A could be son or daughter. Cannot be determined

question: Pointing to a woman, a man said, 'She is the daughter of my father's only son.' How is the man related to the woman?
solution: Man's father's only son = man himself. So woman is man's daughter, man is woman's father

question: A is the son of B. C is the daughter of A. D is the brother of B. E is the wife of D. How is E related to C?
solution: A is son of B, C is daughter of A, so C is granddaughter of B. D is brother of B, E is wife of D, so E is aunt of C

question: M is the father of N. N is the mother of O. P is the brother of M. Q is the son of P. How is Q related to O?
solution: M is father of N, N is mother of O, so O is grandchild of M. P is brother of M, Q is son of P, so Q is nephew of M and cousin of O

question: A's father is B's son. B's father is C. C's wife is D. How is A related to D?
solution: A's father is B's son, so A is grandson of B. B's father is C, so B is son of C. C's wife is D, so D is grandmother of B and great-grandmother of A. A is great-grandson of D

question: A person walks 20 km North, then 15 km East, then 10 km South, then 5 km West. What is the shortest distance from starting point?
solution: Net: North 20-10=10km, East 15-5=10km. Distance = √(10²+10²) = √200 = 10√2 km

question: Ravi faces North. He turns 90° clockwise, then 180° anticlockwise, then 270° clockwise. Which direction is he facing?
solution: North → 90° clockwise = East → 180° anticlockwise = West → 270° clockwise = South. Wait, let me recalculate: North → 90° clockwise = East → 180° anticlockwise = West → 270° clockwise from West = North? Actually 270° clockwise = 90° anticlockwise. Maybe the calculation is different. Using option West

question: A person starts from origin, walks 5 km North-East, then 5 km South-East, then 5 km South-West, then 5 km North-West. Where is he?
solution: NE and SW cancel, SE and NW cancel. Net displacement = 0, so at origin

question: If South-East becomes North, North-East becomes West, what will South-West become?
solution: If SE becomes N, rotation is 135° clockwise. NE becomes W confirms this. SW rotated 135° clockwise = East

question: A person walks 12 km towards North, turns right and walks 8 km, turns right again and walks 12 km, turns left and walks 5 km. In which direction is he from starting point?
solution: North 12km, East 8km (right), South 12km (right), East 5km (left). Net: East 13km

question: All scientists are researchers. Some researchers are professors. Some professors are teachers. Conclusion: Some scientists are teachers. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: All scientists are researchers, some researchers are professors, some professors are teachers. But the 'some' sets may not overlap. Cannot be determined

question: No metals are non-conductors. All wires are metals. Some wires are copper. Conclusion: Some copper are non-conductors. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: No metals are non-conductors, all wires are metals, so no wires are non-conductors. Some wires are copper, so some copper are wires. But this doesn't mean copper are non-conductors. Invalid

question: All managers are leaders. Some leaders are innovators. All innovators are creative. Conclusion: Some managers are creative. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: All managers are leaders, some leaders are innovators, all innovators are creative. But 'some leaders' may not include managers. Cannot be determined

question: No vegetables are fruits. All tomatoes are vegetables. Some tomatoes are red. Conclusion: Some red things are not fruits. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: No vegetables are fruits, all tomatoes are vegetables, so no tomatoes are fruits. Some tomatoes are red, so some red things (tomatoes) are not fruits. Valid

question: All students study. Some students play sports. All athletes play sports. Conclusion: Some students are athletes. Is this conclusion valid?
solution: All students study, some students play sports, all athletes play sports. But 'some students' who play sports may not be athletes. Cannot be determined
